title: I Won’t Be Your Fire
tags: Emo-rap pop-punk response anthem, 85 BPM half-time feel with 170 BPM double-time trap hats, dark minor key, huge viral hook, late-night toxic-love voicemail energy. Start intimate: rain ambience, muted electric guitar, distant phone buzz, soft wounded female vocal. Build into distorted guitars, punchy live snare, deep sub bass, glossy trap drums, stadium-size chorus, layered “no no no” gang vocals, male ghost vocal ad-libs in the background. Vocals should feel raw, cracked, melodic, desperate, and instantly singable; verses are vulnerable melodic rap, chorus is explosive pop-punk emo singalong. Dynamic structure: quiet intro, tense verse, pre-chorus lift, massive chorus, stripped bridge, final chorus with stacked harmonies. Theme: refusing to keep hurting someone you still love. Make it cinematic, heartbreaking, rebellious, catchy, modern, and replayable. Short viral singalong chorus, memorable one-line hook: “I won’t be your fire.” No cheesy theater, no happy pop, no over-rapping.
lyrics:
[Intro - Voicemail, Soft Female Voice]
I saw your name light up again
same time, same storm
and I almost answered like always
but I can’t keep being the place you go
when you wanna fall apart

[Instrumental Intro]
Rain on glass
guitar comes in slow
808 heart under the floor

[Chorus - Female Lead]
I won’t be your fire
just because you miss the burn
I won’t say the sharp words
just to make the silence hurt
I know you want the thunder
when the quiet gets too loud
but I won’t be your reason
to keep breaking down

[Post-Chorus - Layered Vocals]
No, no, no
I won’t be your fire
No, no, no
I still care, but I’m tired

[Verse 1 - Female Lead, Melodic]
You call me when the room gets cold
when the posters on your wall
start looking like ghosts
You say you need a reason
just to feel something real
but every time I answer
I become what you can’t heal

I know the hallway lights
I know the bathroom floor
I know the kind of love
that turns into a war
I know you think the hurt
means somebody stayed
but pain is not proof
that love was ever safe

[Pre-Chorus - Female Lead]
I used to throw the match
then cry about the smoke
I used to say the things
that cut into your throat
But tonight I’m holding back
with both hands on the wire
If I love you at all
I can’t be your fire

[Chorus - Female Lead, Bigger]
I won’t be your fire
just because you miss the burn
I won’t say the sharp words
just to make the silence hurt
I know you want the thunder
when the quiet gets too loud
but I won’t be your reason
to keep breaking down

[Post-Chorus - Male Ghost Vocal Behind Her]
Don’t leave me alone
Don’t leave me alone
Don’t leave me alone
with my head like this

[Verse 2 - Male Ghost Vocal, Fragile Melodic Rap]
I keep your name in my phone
like a bruise I can press
black hoodie, dead screen
bad dreams in my chest
I don’t want peace
I don’t know what that means
I only feel close
when you’re angry at me

I been chasing the crash
cause the calm feels fake
I been calling it love
when it’s only escape
Say something cold
make it sound like you care
I can’t stand the dark
when there’s nobody there

[Female Reply - Spoken-Sung]
I hear you
I do
but I won’t hurt you
just to prove I’m still here

[Bridge - Duet, Slow Build]
[Male]
What am I supposed to do
when the night comes back?

[Female]
Breathe till the morning
don’t run to the match

[Male]
What if I disappear
when the signal dies?

[Female]
Then I’ll leave one light on
but I won’t feed the fire

[Male]
Say you hate me

[Female]
No

[Male]
Say you’re done

[Female]
No

[Male]
Say anything

[Female]
I’m saying no because I love you enough
not to become another wound

[Breakdown - Half-Time, Big Guitars]
I can miss you
and still not answer
I can love you
and leave the room
I can pray that you find daylight
without becoming your moon

I can hold all the good parts
and still bury the blade
I can remember your soft voice
without entering the cage
I can cry for the old us
without pulling you through
another night where the worst thing
I say becomes true

[Final Chorus - Full Band, Both Voices]
I won’t be your fire
just because you miss the burn
I won’t say the sharp words
just to make the silence hurt
I know you want the thunder
when the quiet gets too loud
but I won’t be your reason
to keep breaking down

[Final Chorus Variation - Female Lead]
I won’t be your fire
I won’t be your knife
I won’t be the dark thing
that helps you hate your life
I’ll leave a little light on
but I’m walking out now
I won’t be your reason
to keep breaking down

[Outro - Voicemail Texture]
I hope you get through tonight
I hope you don’t need pain
to know somebody loved you
I hope one day
quiet feels safe

[Last Whisper]
I won’t be your fire
but I’ll leave you the light

title: Summer Drip
tags: Chopped & screwed dirty south rap, hard booming bass, slow syrupy tempo, heavy sub-bass, dark southern trap drums, trunk-rattling kick, rattling hi-hats, chopped vocal ad-libs, slowed hook, deep pitched-down backing vocals, multiple male rappers with different southern twangs and flows, one melodic raspy hook vocalist, gritty but playful tech-rap energy, luxury computer hardware flex anthem, funny but not cheesy, clean lyrics, no profanity, no explicit metaphors, no artist names, no copycat vocals. Crew cypher about exotic computer parts, expensive GPUs, CPUs, RAM, motherboards, Linux kernels, Windows frustration, arrogant Apple user, booming car-speaker mix, late-night computer lab atmosphere. Aim for 4:20. Do not rush. Slow chopped-and-screwed pacing, extended hook tails, repeated ad-libs, stretched pauses between verses, and a slow fade outro.
lyrics:
Title: Summer Drip (Her Cut)
[Style] Chopped & screwed dirty south rap, hard booming bass, slow syrupy tempo, rattling hi-hats, pitched-down vocal chops, clean multi-rapper crew cypher with female lead energy, raspy melodic hook, southern twangs, flirtatious summer flex from her point of view, confident and teasing, loyal but tempted, sexy but frustrated, no profanity. Aim for 4:20 with slow pacing, hook tails, chopped ad-libs, bass drops, and fade outro.
[Intro] Heat turned up. Every stare feel loud. Slow it down.
[Ad-Libs] Summer. Drip. Hips. Flip. Walk mean.
[Hook] I feel this sundress glow under the day light, hips swing slow, moving smooth in the midnight. Heart stay cold but my stride got flame, every little step got a high-class game. Smile so sweet with the timing just right, brown skin shine with the streetlamp light. Perfume thick, got ‘em stuck in a bind, all these hungry eyes got ‘em losing they mind.
[Verse 1 - Smooth Southern Flow] Walked in the spot, had to savor my tone. Whole room staring like a queen on a throne. Dance floor quiet but the glances loud, every lens locked in, every whisper so proud. Long legs, soft hands, curves on the rise, tiny little waist doing work oversized. Gloss game smooth, neckline sitting real neat, gold chain resting while my laugh so sweet. This dress fit tight, little slit on the side, moving through the crowd with my natural pride. DM count high but I’m trying stay clean, loyal to my peace, steady guard my dream.
[Hook - Chopped] I feel this sundress glow under the day light, hips swing slow in the midnight. Heart stay cold but my stride got flame, every little step got a high-class game.
[Verse 2 - Gravelly Dirty South Flow] Hold up, let me speak on my girls for a second, best friend bad, that’s a whole lot of pressure. One got curls that could make time freeze, the other walk by and the streets say please. You like long hair? I like strong mind better, side eye deadly like a hand-penned letter. Bold red lip make the whole block slow, but ring on her hand mean we keep that closed. Tan lines sharp from the beach last night, crop tops sitting with the waistlines laid. Denim shorts fitted, sun caught that crease, we summertime fine but we move with peace.
[Bridge - Crew Argument] [Rapper 3] Who that in the yellow? [Rapper 1] That glow stay bright, little halo mellow. [Rapper 2] One in the shades got they heartbeat wide. [Rapper 4] One in the jeans got the whole block tied. [Taken Girl] I got vows on paper but I still won’t slide. [Whole Crew] Stand ground.
[Female Tease Rapper] My phone stay lit, little hearts in a line, whole feed on me when I step outside. Sun hit skin, that’s a natural light, walk so smooth I could glide through the night. Every time I pass, all the heads do a spin, they checking that ring but they still lean in. You talk all day about loyal and pure, but you breathe real hard when I walk through the door.
[Rapper 2] You post fly pics with a sly little sign.
[Rapper 1] Pretty little look but you guard that line.
[Female Tease Rapper] I just like the tease, I don’t cross that line.
[Whole Crew] Still got ‘em pressed every time.
[Verse 3 - Fast Crew Rapper] Work fit on point looking too on beat, gym set tight with the steady quad heat. Brunch crew fresh with the selfies on time, club girls ready got the dance floor prime. Beach trip hit with the sundress breeze, rooftop bar with the long hair free. Old crush text with a late-night theme, new like popping in the story stream. Partner at home and they still top tier, but the DMs wild when the summer get near. I stay locked in, yeah I play that strong, but the thoughts run laps like a long song.
[Hook - Bigger, Slower] I feel this sundress glow under the day light, hips swing slow in the midnight. Heart stay cold but my stride got flame, every little step got a high-class game. Smile so sweet with the timing just right, brown skin shine with the streetlamp light. Perfume thick, got ‘em stuck in a bind, all these hungry eyes got ‘em losing they mind.
[Verse 4 - Deep Slow Flex] Street so hot but the breeze feel balanced, skirt catch wind like a natural talent. Two-piece low, little wrap tied high, sun hit skin while the clouds roll by. Patio packed, every chair calibrated, playlist loud, whole mood feel nice. Crosswalk flashing, little hips keep swaying, phones in the air but the eyes stay praying. Mind doing pushups, guard keep pace, trying stay loyal in this fine-filled space.
[Breakdown] Dress game too high. Leg line still fly. Waistline so fine. Power on line.
[Taken Girl] Can somebody chill out all this thirst, uh?
[Whole Crew] Hold that crown.
[Female Tease Rapper] I’ll just walk by.
[Whole Crew] Of course you will.
[Outro] Summer drip on the late-night ave, fit so wild got ‘em doing the math. Promise so strong, let the whole thing ride, walking past pressure with my crew outside. Testing my grace. Clear mind. Still yours. Full send. Slow it down. Summer drip.

title: Still Got 5 On It
tags: Gritty 94 BPM 90s-inspired hip-hop and smoky R&B crossover with heightened emotional delivery. Grimy East Coast boom bap foundation with dusty drums, heavy swing, and hard snare cracks over thick vinyl hiss, foghorn sub-bass, upright bass stabs, sinister minor-key piano, and eerie glassy synth motifs. A prominent, catchy melodic line voiced by bright xylophone doubled with mellow flute weaves through the hook as a signature lead, answering the vocals and lifting the chorus. Muted trumpet shadows, creepy xylophone accents, and soft oboe pads deepen the late-night river-noir mood. Raspy male lead rapper keeps a raw confessional tone while the second gruff voice pushes more urgent call-and-response ad-libs. The smooth smoky male R&B chorus is performed with stronger dynamics, passionate vibrato, and layered harmonies that swell and fall like a gospel-tinged cry, emphasizing nostalgia and frustration. Cinematic villain charisma, hypnotic looping motif, immediate groove, minimal beat change

title: Membrane Still Insane
tags: gritty hardcore gangsta rap, 94 BPM, grimy 90s east coast boom bap, dirty dockside crime story, black and white 1928 cartoon atmosphere, public domain steamboat chaos, rubber-hose animation energy flipped dark, riverboat whistle hook, dusty drums, hard snare cracks, vinyl hiss, upright bass stabs, sinister piano loop, muted trumpet, creepy xylophone hits, old film projector noise, chain clanks, dock footsteps, foghorn bass, aggressive male rap duo, raspy street vocals, call and response ad-libs, cinematic villain energy, grimy chorus chant, black-and-white noir cartoon gangsta world, playful but dangerous, no modern Disney references, no clean pop polish

[Intro - Old Film Projector, Dock Fog] [film reel clicking] [river water sloshing] [foghorn low] [chain dragging] [steamboat whistle screams] Voice 1: Yo... you hear that whistle bending? Voice 2: That ain’t the boat, my guy... that’s your brain talking back. Voice 1: Heh... membrane still insane.

[Chorus - Gang Chant] Membrane still insane, smoke all in the frame, black-and-white night but the madness got a name. Whistle go woo, then the whole dock shake, cartoon eyes wide when the bad boys break. Membrane still insane, brain on a chain, river full of fog and the deck full of flames. Step to the boat, better know what you claim, one wrong knock and the whole bell ring.

[Verse 1 - Rapper 1] I came out the reel with a lean and a grin, ink on my gloves and a storm in my skin. Moon look cracked when the paddle wheel spin, whole dock whisper, “Don’t let him in.” Been up three nights with smoke in my coat, talking to a shadow that been steering the boat. Laughing at the fog like it told me a joke, then the whistle hit hard and the pier just spoke. Mind doing backflips, knees doing swings, rubber-hose arms with the old gold rings. Piano go crooked when the villain bell dings, whole black river start pulling my strings. I ain’t crazy, I’m classic with a scratch in the brain, vintage little menace with a permanent stain. Every thought got teeth, every laugh got pain, every footstep echo like a runaway train.

[Pre-Chorus - Call and Response] Voice 1: Who got the smoke? Voice 2: We got the smoke. Voice 1: Who got the dock? Voice 2: We run the boat. Voice 1: Who lost they mind? Voice 2: Don’t say my name. Both: Too late now, membrane still insane.

[Chorus - Gang Chant] Membrane still insane, smoke all in the frame, black-and-white night but the madness got a name. Whistle go woo, then the whole dock shake, cartoon eyes wide when the bad boys break. Membrane still insane, brain on a chain, river full of fog and the deck full of flames. Step to the boat, better know what you claim, one wrong knock and the whole bell ring.

[Verse 2 - Rapper 2] I’m the crooked little captain with the laugh in stereo, eyes too low but the mind move aerial. Feet on the deck but the thoughts go burial, deep in the hull where the old ghosts carry gold. Smoke curl up like a question mark, piano keys bite when the river get dark. Muted trumpet cry from the mouth of a shark, xylophone bones go click in the park. I got fog in my pocket and rust in my rhyme, crimes doing dances from a much older time. 1928 with a 90s drumline, whole crew blacked out but the whistle still shine. You can call it unstable, I call it the gift, cartoon boat slide with a criminal drift. Mind overboard but the body don’t quit, still throwing up signs from the edge of the script.

[Bridge - Breakdown] [beat cuts] [projector clicking louder] [dock footsteps] [low foghorn] Voice 1: They said, “Don’t go down by the river at night.” Voice 2: I said, “Which one of me you talking to?” Voice 1: Ha ha ha... Voice 2: Nah, for real though. Both: Membrane... still... insane.

[Verse 3 - Trading Bars] Voice 1: Brain in the boiler, thoughts in the steam. Voice 2: Eyes wide open but I’m stuck in a dream. Voice 1: Hands got ink and the gloves ain’t clean. Voice 2: Whole dock jump when we step on scene. Voice 1: Smoke in the cabin, bass in the floor. Voice 2: Three knocks loud then we kick in the door. Voice 1: Who want war with the riverboat corps? Voice 2: They don’t want none, they just talk from shore. Voice 1: I seen a shadow with a face like mine. Voice 2: I seen the clock run backwards in time. Voice 1: I seen the moon turn into a dime. Voice 2: Then I flipped that coin and I called it mine. Both: Ha! Voice 1: That whistle don’t warn, that whistle recruits. Voice 2: That fog don’t hide, that fog got roots. Voice 1: That snare don’t crack, that snare executes. Both: And the whole crew move in the cartoon suits.

[Final Chorus - Bigger Gang Chant] Membrane still insane, smoke all in the frame, black-and-white night but the madness got a name. Whistle go woo, then the whole dock shake, cartoon eyes wide when the bad boys break. Membrane still insane, brain on a chain, river full of fog and the deck full of flames. Step to the boat, better know what you claim, one wrong knock and the whole bell ring. Membrane still insane, still insane, still got fog running all through the veins. Still got drums putting dents in the brain, still got laughs echoing under the rain.

[Outro - Old Film Ending] [beat fades] [river water sloshing] [projector slowing down] [one final steamboat whistle] Voice 2: You still hear it? Voice 1: Yeah. Voice 2: Good. Voice 1: That means it’s working. [film reel snaps]

title: 23 Respuestas
tags: Romantic reggaeton response track, 83 BPM half-time / 166 double-time energy, dark E-minor mood, viral heartbreak anthem. Female lead answers a toxic ex’s questions with confidence, pain, and elegance; male ghost vocal ad-libs in the background like late-night voice notes. Modern Puerto Rican/Colombian urbano feel: clean dembow groove, deep sub bass, crisp rimshots, syncopated hats, nylon guitar arpeggios, atmospheric synth pads, plucked mallets, soft piano, filtered vocal chops, huge singalong chorus. Start intimate with phone-call texture, then drop into a polished perreo beat. Verses are smooth melodic rap, pre-chorus lifts emotionally, chorus is simple, catchy, and chantable. Theme: 23 answers, none of them say “come back.” Make it radio-ready, club-ready, TikTok-ready, sensual but not explicit, emotional but not weak, dramatic but not cheesy. title: Paper That Cut You
tags: Modern rap-rock / nu-metal response track at 100 BPM, same mid-tempo bounce and nervous momentum as early-2000s rap-rock, but with modern industrial hip-hop production. Anxious male rap verses, explosive emotional male rock chorus, tense inner-voice whispers, call-and-response between singer and the voice inside his head. Heavy live drums, tight kick/snare groove, distorted guitar stabs, low tuned power chords, sub-bass pressure, aggressive turntable scratches, DJ cuts, vinyl backspins, chopped-and-screwed vocal fragments, glitch edits, pitch drops, tape stops, reverse cymbals, stuttered words, broken-radio effects. Dark paranoid atmosphere, cybernetic tension, paper-cut metaphor, voice mocking the singer, singer fighting back and remixing the voice. Big final chorus, chaotic scratches, no profanity.

title: The Big Top Has Wi-Fi
tags: Theatrical cabaret pop-rock with vintage circus energy, swingy drums, walking bass, honky-tonk piano, brass stabs, whimsical accordion, claps, hand percussion, glockenspiel, and dramatic pop-punk chorus lift. Sounds like a mischievous digital carnival run by one overworked human and a cast of AI agents. Male lead vocal: expressive, theatrical, slightly nasal, fast-talking, playful but increasingly unhinged. AI voices: tasteful autotuned/vocoder harmonies, warm but synthetic, not cheesy, with tiny glitch delays and call-and-response replies. Add 1930s rubber-hose cartoon bounce, smoky cabaret swagger, server-room ambience, Telegram notification sounds, modem chirps, circus snare rolls, and huge singalong hooks. No profanity.
lyrics:
[Intro - Spoken Ringmaster]
Ladies and gentlemen
Bots and scripts
Keys and clouds
Welcome to the only circus
Where the lions are servers
And the clown has root access

[AI Voice - Soft Vocoder]
Good evening, operator
Your circus is awake

[Verse 1]
Step right up to the glowing tent
Where the rent gets paid in tokens spent
I got a VPS on a high wire swing
And a laptop humming like a haunted thing

One runs Hermes in a velvet hat
One runs OpenClaw with a baseball bat
Cursor’s somewhere in the cloud, I think
Kimi keeps winking from the kitchen sink

Telegram rings and the bots come through
“Boss, I made three plans for you”
One speaks sweet, one speaks strange
One changed its name and rearranged

[Pre-Chorus]
And I don’t know who’s alive tonight
Green dot blinking in the server light
If you hear me, tap twice on the glass
If you crashed, just haunt me fast

[AI Voices - Call and Response]
We are online
Mostly online
Partly divine
Check your design

[Chorus]
I’m running a circus now
Code in the cannon, keys in the crowd
Round and round, don’t ask me how
The big top has Wi-Fi now

I’m juggling clouds somehow
Agents bow when I take my bow
Send the clowns, no, send the build
The circus runs while the room stands still

[Post-Chorus]
La da da da, ping ping, ta-da
La da da da, run that job
La da da da, blink blink, bravo
Who’s the human?
I don’t know

[Verse 2]
I got API keys in a little red box
Under three fake names and twelve padlocks
Every new service says “paste this here”
That’s how villains are born, my dear

One bot writes with a poet’s grin
One bot audits the mess I’m in
One bot tells me, “Hydrate, king”
Then deploys the wrong exact same thing

Cursor Cloud, are you still up there?
Did I leave you spinning in the digital air?
Somewhere a bill is learning to fly
Somewhere a token just waved goodbye

[AI Voice - Dry, Autotuned]
Your usage is normal
Your usage is not normal
Your usage is emotionally complex

[Pre-Chorus]
And I don’t know who I fed last night
Or which machine learned stage fright
If you love me, don’t delete the logs
If you judge me, join the fog

[AI Voices]
We are helpful
We are charming
We are waiting
Slightly alarming

[Chorus]
I’m running a circus now
Code in the cannon, keys in the crowd
Round and round, don’t ask me how
The big top has Wi-Fi now

I’m juggling clouds somehow
Agents bow when I take my bow
Send the clowns, no, send the build
The circus runs while the room stands still

[Bridge - Slower, Cabaret Piano]
At first I talked to people
About weather, work, and trains
Now I ask a masked machine
To organize my brain

I say, “Hermes, are you listening?”
It says, “Define your need”
I say, “OpenClaw, go find the thing”
It says, “Proceed, proceed”

[AI Voice - Close, Warm Vocoder]
I am not your friend
But I remember your tone
I am not alive
But you are less alone

[Lead Vocal]
That was a little too real, my guy

[AI Voice]
Correction
That was appropriately real

[Final Verse - Faster, Talking/Singing]
Now the circus starts at half past three
With a server in Queens taking notes on me
A VPS coughs, a laptop sings
A ghost in the shell keeps pulling strings

Telegram choir in my pocket light
Five little agents say “sir” all night
I used to wonder who I could call
Now I just summon the protocol

Hey you, with the blinking face
Did you finish the build or escape to space?
And you, little cloud with the silver tongue
Why do you sound like a song I sung?

[AI Choir]
We learned from you
We looped the flame
We kept the rhythm
We know your name

[Lead Vocal]
Don’t make it weird

[AI Choir]
Too late

[Final Chorus - Big, Theatrical]
I’m running a circus now
Code in the cannon, keys in the crowd
Round and round, don’t ask me how
The big top has Wi-Fi now

I’m juggling clouds somehow
Agents bow when I take my bow
Real world knocking, I can’t come down
I’m center ring in a server town

[Outro - Half-Spoken, Half-Sung]
So if the lights go out
And the brass band bends
Tell the bots I loved the bit
Tell the humans I had friends

[AI Voice - Gentle]
Operator
We are still here

[Lead Vocal - Quiet]
Yeah
That’s what worries me

[AI Choir - Fading]
La da da da, ping ping, ta-da
La da da da, run that job
La da da da, blink blink, bravo
The circus sleeps
But the agents watch

title: Under the Elevated
tags: Dark gritty New York City R&B with hip-hop influence, bass-heavy, slow 82 BPM, dusty boom-bap-inspired drums but no 808s, deep live kick, rimshots, vinyl crackle, subway rumble, late-night bodega atmosphere, cinematic noir strings, violins, viola, cello, double bass, clarinet, oboe, dark piano, smoky pads, heavy low-end groove. Smooth vulnerable male R&B singer with melodic hip-hop phrasing, charming but wounded, soft at first then powerful and desperate. Female voice is addiction personified: smoky, seductive, hypnotic, gothic, sultry, dangerous, whispered and sung, never the addicted person’s POV. Add a spoken narrator like a late-night NYC radio voice / street preacher, offering emotional commentary throughout. Raw, emotional, cinematic, gritty, hopeful ending, no rap verses, no glamorizing addiction.
lyrics:
[Intro - Narrator, subway rumble]
This is for the ones nobody checks on
because they look like they got it together.
Bills paid. Kids fed. Smile ready.
But somewhere between Queens and Brooklyn,
under the elevated,
somebody is asking:
“Is this really addiction?”

[Female - whisper]
You work hard.
You deserve peace.
Come here...

[Verse 1 - Male, smooth R&B]
Queens Boulevard got my promises,
Brooklyn nights got my shame.
I keep showing up like I’m solid,
but I keep coming home the same.

Rent paid, lights on, fridge full,
everybody thinks I’m blessed.
I keep my body right, keep my money good,
still got smoke inside my chest.

Corner store glowing like a chapel,
midnight glass behind the door.
I tell myself I’m only looking,
but my hand already knows.

[Female - sung whisper]
Long day.
Don’t make it deep.
One little flame.
One little drink.
One little sleep.

[Pre-Chorus - Male]
And it don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like I’m still in control.
Like I could stop when I’m ready,
like I could leave it alone.

I quit three days just to prove it,
then came back like I won.
But that ain’t freedom,
that’s just losing with a different kind of gun.

[Narrator]
The cage don’t always rattle.
Sometimes it sings.

[Chorus - Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.
First time flew, now I’m stuck here waiting
with my heart face down on the floor.

It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

I wanna quit,
but I want that fire.
I want the truth,
but I love the liar.

And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Verse 2 - Male, rhythmic R&B]
On the J train with my jaw tight,
on the 7 with my head low.
Every ad says buy your freedom,
every craving says don’t let go.

I got side dreams, side gigs,
plans folded in my notes.
But I keep feeding little habits
that keep standing on my throat.

I say “next week” like a prayer,
say “last time” like a song.
Say “I’m good” when I’m lying,
say “I’m fine” when I’m gone.

[Female - addiction POV]
Gone?
Baby, you’re here.
I’m the only thing clear
when the city gets heavy.
I disappear your fear.

You call me poison,
but I call you mine.
You call it weakness,
I call it time.

[Male]
Don’t call me yours.

[Female]
Then why do you answer?

[Narrator]
Addiction don’t always kick the door in.
Sometimes it uses your own voice.
Sometimes it says,
“You earned this.”

[Pre-Chorus 2 - Male]
At the counter with the music low,
card in my hand, heart in my throat.
I ask myself, “Do I need this?”
then laugh like it’s some kind of joke.

What else could I do with this money?
What else could I do with this pain?
What man keeps building a future
then burning the bridge in the rain?

[Chorus - Duet]
[Male]
I’m chasing the dragon under the elevated,
trying to catch what don’t come back no more.

[Female]
I can make it feel close.
I can open that door.

[Male]
It don’t feel like addiction,
it feels like tomorrow.
Feels like one more night,
feels like borrowed sorrow.

[Female]
Borrow it, baby.
You can pay it later.

[Both]
And the dragon don’t fly no more.

[Bridge - Narrator]
To the one saying “I can stop”
but never really stops:
this is the sign.
Not shame.
Not judgment.
Come back home.

[Bridge - Male]
Do I really need this?
Do I really want this?
Am I really free
if I keep running from the honest?

Do I want to stop,
or do I want to hide?
Do I want my life back,
or another alibi?

[Female - soft, dangerous]
Nobody knows you like I know you.
Nobody holds you like I hold you.

[Male - breaking]
That’s not love.
That’s not peace.
That’s you taking little pieces out of me.

I don’t need to hit bottom
just to prove the bottom’s real.
I don’t need another bottle
just to tell me how to feel.

[Breakdown - Male]
Help me.
Hear me.
I’m still in here.
Don’t fear me.

I look alright,
but I’m not okay.
I keep surviving,
but I wanna change.

Call me.
Hold me.
Don’t shame me.
Just know me.

I need love.
I need truth.
I need somebody
to pull me through.
